metadata.project_name: Crypto Analytics and ML Signal Platform
metadata.purpose: Static visual guide for recruiters and reviewers.
scope_notes: This is an analytics and ML signal platform, not a trading bot.
The page is documentation-only and does not run ingestion, training, deployment, or cloud writes.
MLflow, Optuna, and MLflow Registry are optional and off by default.
subset9 and microstructure features are research/manual candidates, not production defaults.

coverage.summary: Reliable 5-year backfill coverage is currently strongest for Binance trades, ETF indicators, macro indicators, and funding data. Other sources are partial, experimental, or not fully live-ready.

tabs.label: Platform Overview
tabs.title: Platform Overview
tabs.summary: Guided view of how batch and streaming ingestion, dbt, orchestration, runtime infrastructure, CI/CD, and conservative ML signal workflows fit together.

cards.title: End-to-End Scope
cards.body: Ingestion, dbt marts, orchestration, MLOps controls, monitoring, CI/CD, and deployment gates are connected in one repo.

cards.title: Conservative Runtime
cards.body: Production prediction is artifact-first. Registry, Optuna, and research candidates stay opt-in.

cards.title: Explicit Data Coverage
cards.body: Best historical confidence: Binance trades, ETF, macro, and funding. Other feeds are partial.

important_files.label: Project Overview
important_files.note: Top-level project summary and safety notes.

important_files.label: Architecture Guide
important_files.note: End-to-end platform architecture and operational boundaries.

important_files.label: Repository Map
important_files.note: Folder-by-folder reviewer guide.

warnings: Do not present the system as automated trading infrastructure.
Do not infer model edge from partial source coverage.

tabs.label: Batch Ingestion
tabs.title: Batch Ingestion and Trusted Backfills
tabs.summary: Most mature ingestion path for trusted collectors, selected historical backfills, validation, and downstream analytics/ML preparation.

cards.title: Trusted Five-Year Coverage
cards.body: Reliable 5-year backfill is strongest for Binance trades, ETF indicators, macro indicators, and funding data.

cards.title: Backfill Discipline
cards.body: Historical loaders can be expensive and may write to cloud targets, so they should run only when intentionally configured.

cards.title: Validation Before Modeling
cards.body: Rule-based validation and quality audit paths help catch schema, freshness, and data-quality issues before dbt and ML consume the data.

important_files.label: Main Collectors
important_files.note: Collectors for trusted and experimental batch sources.

important_files.label: Backfill Scripts
important_files.note: Historical loaders for selected trusted sources.

important_files.label: Validation Rules
important_files.note: Validation engine and YAML rulesets.

important_files.label: Quality Audit Specs
important_files.note: Great Expectations-style specs for dashboard, ML, and freshness checks.

important_files.label: Iceberg / BigLake Path
important_files.note: Lakehouse-oriented loader path for curated storage workflows.

docs.label: Batch Pipeline Docs
docs.note: Beginner-friendly guide to collectors, backfills, validation, and safety.

warnings: Do not run backfills casually.
Avoid documenting or linking local .env files, service account keys, local output data, or Terraform state.

tabs.label: Streaming Pipeline
tabs.title: Streaming Pipeline and Freshness Path
tabs.summary: Lower-latency producer and Flink/Kafka-oriented transformation area for freshness experiments; still partial compared with trusted batch coverage.

cards.title: Producer Layer
cards.body: Market, on-chain, and sentiment producers prepare lower-latency context for streaming experiments.

cards.title: Flink Transformations
cards.body: Transformation modules shape recent signals such as order-flow, breakout, liquidation, and dead-letter handling.

cards.title: Experimental Boundary
cards.body: Streaming is useful for freshness work, but it is not the primary full-history training coverage path yet.

important_files.label: Streaming Producers
important_files.note: Market, on-chain, and sentiment producers.

important_files.label: Streaming Entrypoint
important_files.note: Flink-oriented transformation entrypoint.

important_files.label: Transformation Modules
important_files.note: Signal transformation modules and dead-letter handling.

important_files.label: Sink Specs and Helpers
important_files.note: BigQuery sink specs and local deployment helper scripts; avoid secrets.

docs.label: Streaming Pipeline Docs
docs.note: Beginner-friendly guide to producers, Kafka/Flink concepts, and freshness boundaries.

warnings: Validate topic, connector, sink, and freshness health before tying streaming output to automatic prediction.
Do not expose local connector credentials or key files in documentation.

tabs.label: dbt Transformation Layers
tabs.title: dbt Transformation Layers
tabs.summary: Layered BigQuery models normalize sources, align time-series features, and publish dashboard, ML, and monitoring marts.

cards.title: Readable Lineage
cards.body: Staging normalizes sources, intermediate models align features, and marts publish dashboard, ML, and monitoring contracts.

cards.title: ML Contract Boundary
cards.body: dbt may expose additive research columns, but production training only uses the conservative ml/feature_list.yml contract.

important_files.label: dbt Project Config
important_files.note: Project-level model configuration.

important_files.label: Staging Models
important_files.note: Source normalization and type cleanup. Technical prefix: stg_*.

important_files.label: Intermediate Models
important_files.note: Hourly/daily alignment and feature aggregation. Technical prefix: int_*.

important_files.label: Core Marts
important_files.note: Reusable analytics facts.

important_files.label: Dashboard Marts
important_files.note: BI-ready outputs.

important_files.label: ML Marts
important_files.note: Training dataset and prediction input.

important_files.label: Monitoring Marts
important_files.note: Freshness, quality, and pipeline health.

docs.label: dbt Model Docs
docs.note: Layer-by-layer guide to staging, intermediate, marts, ML, and monitoring models.

warnings: Avoid label leakage when changing ML marts.
Inspect partitioning, clustering, and materialization before cloud-backed builds.

tabs.label: ML and MLOps Lifecycle
tabs.title: ML and MLOps Lifecycle
tabs.summary: Conservative artifact-first training and prediction with feature contracts, promotion gates, optional MLflow/Optuna/Registry, and research-only scripts kept separate.

cards.title: Default Production Posture
cards.body: Prediction loads artifacts by default. Registry loading is optional and only used when explicitly configured.

cards.title: Research Separation
cards.body: local_*.py scripts support diagnostics, AutoML, ablation, keeper validation, and microstructure experiments without becoming production serving paths.

cards.title: subset9 / Microstructure
cards.body: These features are research/manual candidates and have not replaced the baseline production contract.

important_files.label: Training Entrypoint
important_files.note: Trains models and writes artifacts when configured.

important_files.label: Prediction Entrypoint
important_files.note: Loads latest artifact or optional registry model.

important_files.label: Production Feature Contract
important_files.note: Conservative default contract; do not change casually.

important_files.label: Contract Hashing
important_files.note: Stable metadata for lineage.

important_files.label: Promotion Gate
important_files.note: Prevents automatic promotion of worse candidates.

important_files.label: Model Loader
important_files.note: Artifact-first and optional registry loading.

important_files.label: Local AutoML Research
important_files.note: Research-only local tooling.

important_files.label: Microstructure Contract Trial
important_files.note: Manual research candidate workflow.

docs.label: ML/MLOps Docs
docs.note: Beginner-friendly guide to training, prediction, optional MLOps, and research boundaries.

warnings: MLflow, Optuna, and Registry are optional/off by default.
Do not modify ml/feature_list.yml as part of documentation polish.
Do not run training during documentation-only review.

tabs.label: Kestra Orchestration
tabs.title: Kestra Orchestration
tabs.summary: Flow grouping and orchestration logic for raw, dbt, ML, streaming, monitoring, quality, preview, and master flows. Kubernetes runtime details live in the K8s/GKE tab.

cards.title: Flow Grouping
cards.body: Flows are grouped by workflow intent: raw ingestion, dbt transforms, ML, streaming, monitoring, quality, preview, and master overview.

cards.title: Schedules and Preconditions
cards.body: Production-style flows encode orchestration timing and dependencies, while PR preview flows avoid production triggers.

cards.title: ML Deploy Gate
cards.body: ML flow deployment remains separately gated so ML runtime changes are intentional.

important_files.label: Raw Flows
important_files.note: Batch ingestion snapshots and intraday refresh flow definitions.

important_files.label: dbt Flows
important_files.note: Hourly, intraday, daily market, macro, and ETF transform flows.

important_files.label: ML Flows
important_files.note: Training, prediction, and strategy matrix flow definitions.

important_files.label: Preview Flows
important_files.note: PR validation flows without production triggers.

important_files.label: Master Flow
important_files.note: Overview orchestration flow.

docs.label: Kestra Orchestration Docs
docs.note: Beginner-friendly guide to flow grouping, gates, and orchestration boundaries.

warnings: Do not enable ML deploy simply to make checks green.
Do not add triggers to PR preview flows.

tabs.label: K8s / GKE Runtime
tabs.title: K8s / GKE Runtime
tabs.summary: Runtime layer for production-style jobs and Kestra task execution using GKE Autopilot, Kubernetes pods, Artifact Registry images, Workload Identity, Helm values, and Kubernetes manifests.

cards.title: GKE Runtime Layer
cards.body: GKE provides the Kubernetes runtime where production-style jobs and Kestra task pods can execute.

cards.title: Kubernetes Pod Execution
cards.body: Kestra task execution is separated into pods so batch, dbt, ML, and streaming work can use purpose-built images.

cards.title: Artifact Registry Images
cards.body: Batch, dbt, and ML images are built separately and referenced by orchestration/runtime layers.

cards.title: Workload Identity
cards.body: Cloud access should use Workload Identity and service account bindings instead of committed keys.

cards.title: Cloud SQL Dependency for Kestra
cards.body: Kestra webserver, executor, and worker components may fail if Cloud SQL, required secrets, or identity bindings are not ready.

cards.title: Runtime Safety
cards.body: This runtime layer is infrastructure. It does not define model logic or prove model quality.

important_files.label: Kubernetes Manifests
important_files.note: RBAC and secret-provider support manifests for Kestra runtime.

important_files.label: Kestra Helm Values
important_files.note: GKE-oriented Helm values for Kestra components.

important_files.label: Runtime Docker Images
important_files.note: Batch, dbt, and ML Dockerfiles used by runtime jobs.

important_files.label: GKE Flow Definitions
important_files.note: Kestra flows intended for GKE-oriented execution.

important_files.label: Kestra Deploy Workflow
important_files.note: CI workflow that plans and deploys selected GKE flows.

important_files.label: Deploy Plan Helper
important_files.note: Computes deployment/build gating decisions.

important_files.label: Flow Deploy Helper
important_files.note: Deploys selected flows from CI when gates allow it.

docs.label: K8s / GKE Runtime Docs
docs.note: Runtime guide for Kubernetes, GKE, Helm, images, identity, and Cloud SQL dependencies.

warnings: Do not deploy GKE flows just to make PR checks green.
Kestra components may fail if Cloud SQL, required secrets, or identity bindings are not ready.
ML Kestra deploy remains gated.
GKE runtime is infrastructure/runtime, not model logic.

tabs.label: Terraform Infrastructure
tabs.title: Terraform Infrastructure
tabs.summary: Terraform describes cloud infrastructure such as BigQuery datasets, GCS buckets, Artifact Registry, GKE/Kestra resources, Cloud SQL, IAM, networking, and Grafana support. This docs task does not run terraform apply.

cards.title: Infrastructure as Code
cards.body: Terraform keeps cloud resources described in code so infra can be reviewed instead of configured by memory.

cards.title: GCP Resources
cards.body: The repo includes Terraform for BigQuery, GCS, Artifact Registry, GKE/Kestra, Cloud SQL, networking, and monitoring support.

cards.title: IAM and Service Accounts
cards.body: IAM and service-account definitions support Workload Identity and CI/CD access patterns.

cards.title: Cloud SQL for Kestra
cards.body: Kestra infrastructure includes Cloud SQL-related resources and secrets wiring.

cards.title: Artifact Registry
cards.body: Artifact Registry stores runtime images for batch, dbt, and ML jobs.

cards.title: State and Secrets Safety
cards.body: State files, tfvars with secrets, service account keys, and local credentials must never be committed or exposed.

important_files.label: Main Terraform Module
important_files.note: Primary GCP infrastructure definitions.

important_files.label: Terraform Provider Config
important_files.note: Provider configuration for the main Terraform module.

important_files.label: Main Infrastructure Entry
important_files.note: Main resource composition entrypoint.

important_files.label: Artifact Registry Resources
important_files.note: Container image registry resources.

important_files.label: Kestra GKE Resources
important_files.note: GKE-oriented Kestra runtime infrastructure.

important_files.label: Cloud SQL for Kestra
important_files.note: Cloud SQL resources used by Kestra.

important_files.label: Bootstrap Terraform
important_files.note: Bootstrap IAM/service-account setup.

important_files.label: Grafana Terraform
important_files.note: Grafana-related infrastructure support.

docs.label: Terraform Infrastructure Docs
docs.note: Beginner-friendly guide to infra modules, resources, and safety boundaries.

warnings: Never commit .tfstate, .tfvars with secrets, service account keys, or local credentials.
Do not run terraform apply casually.
Terraform docs are descriptive in this PR only.

tabs.label: CI/CD and Deployment Gates
tabs.title: CI/CD and Deployment Gates
tabs.summary: GitHub Actions, repo guard, Docker gate, Kestra deploy plan, and PR required gate keep docs-only and runtime PRs separated.

cards.title: GitHub Actions Gatekeeping
cards.body: Workflows run quality checks, plan deploy needs, and aggregate required status for PRs.

cards.title: Docker Gate
cards.body: Docker image work is gated so docs-only PRs do not build runtime images unnecessarily.

cards.title: Kestra Deploy Plan
cards.body: Deploy planning decides which flow groups and runtime checks are relevant without explaining Kubernetes runtime internals.

cards.title: Docs-Only Safety
cards.body: Documentation changes should not trigger training, backfills, deploys, cloud writes, or Docker builds.

important_files.label: Quality Checks
important_files.note: Tests, repo guard, and static validation workflow.

important_files.label: Docker Build Gate
important_files.note: Gated Docker build and smoke workflow.

important_files.label: Kestra Deploy Gate
important_files.note: Deploys selected flows only when plan allows it.

important_files.label: PR Required Gate
important_files.note: Aggregates required PR check status.

important_files.label: Kestra Deploy Plan
important_files.note: Computes deploy/build gating outputs.

important_files.label: Repository Guard
important_files.note: Checks repository safety rules.

docs.label: CI/CD Gates Docs
docs.note: Beginner-friendly guide to workflows, gating, and docs-only safety.

warnings: Docs-only changes should not require training, backfills, deploys, or cloud writes.
Secrets, local artifacts, and Terraform state must stay out of docs and commits.

tabs.label: Repository Map
tabs.title: Repository Map
tabs.summary: Quick map for ingestion, streaming, dbt, orchestration, ML, infrastructure, CI/CD, Docker, tests, and docs.

cards.title: Fast Review Path
cards.body: Start with README.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/repository_map.md, then use this explorer to understand subsystem boundaries.

cards.title: Sensitive Files Omitted
cards.body: Local env files, key files, Terraform state, logs, generated outputs, and local artifacts are intentionally excluded from this explorer.

important_files.label: Full Repository Map
important_files.note: Canonical folder-by-folder description.

important_files.label: Repo Guard
important_files.note: Safety checks for repository hygiene.

docs.label: Repository Map Docs
docs.note: Grouped repo guide by subsystem, purpose, type, and caution.

warnings: Avoid linking secret-like files or local generated artifacts.
Treat Terraform state and tfvars as sensitive operational files.

tabs.label: Production Boundaries
tabs.title: Production Boundaries
tabs.summary: Production entrypoints and feature contracts stay separate from local research scripts, experimental features, and optional MLOps.

cards.title: Production Default Files
cards.body: The production side stays conservative: explicit feature list, contract hashing, training/prediction entrypoints, promotion gate, and artifact-first loading.

cards.title: Research-Only Files
cards.body: Local research scripts support exploration, diagnostics, ablation, recall focus, and keeper validation without becoming serving paths.

cards.title: MLOps Optionality
cards.body: MLflow logging, Optuna tuning, and MLflow Registry integration require explicit configuration and are off by default.

production_files.label: Training Entrypoint
production_files.note: Trains models and writes local/GCS artifacts only when explicitly configured.

production_files.label: Prediction Entrypoint
production_files.note: Loads the latest artifact or optional registry model for signal prediction.

production_files.label: Feature List
production_files.note: Conservative production feature contract; not changed by docs work.

production_files.label: Feature Contract
production_files.note: Hashes and validates the feature contract for reproducible lineage.

production_files.label: Promotion Gate
production_files.note: Prevents automatic promotion of weaker candidate models.

production_files.label: Model Loader
production_files.note: Artifact-first model loading with optional registry support.

research_files.label: Local AutoML Research
research_files.note: Local AutoML exploration and candidate discovery.

research_files.label: Feature Label Diagnostics
research_files.note: Feature/label diagnostics for model readiness review.

research_files.label: Feature Engineering Research
research_files.note: Manual feature exploration outside production defaults.

research_files.label: Feature Ablation Research
research_files.note: Ablation experiments for understanding feature contribution.

research_files.label: Down Recall Focus Research
research_files.note: Manual research around downside recall and class behavior.

research_files.label: Keeper Candidate Validation
research_files.note: Manual validation of keeper candidates before any production consideration.

important_files.label: Production Feature List
important_files.note: Not changed by this documentation work.

important_files.label: Optional MLflow Logging
important_files.note: Best-effort unless configured otherwise.

important_files.label: Optional Tuning
important_files.note: Off unless explicitly requested.

important_files.label: Optional Registry
important_files.note: Alias-based registry integration, not required for prediction.

docs.label: Production Boundaries Docs
docs.note: Beginner-friendly guide to production defaults, research-only tooling, and safety boundaries.

warnings: subset9 and microstructure features are research/manual candidates, not production defaults.
This project should not be described as a trading bot.
Do not run training or promotion as part of docs-only review.

tabs.label: Optional AI Codebase Map
tabs.title: Optional AI Codebase Map
tabs.summary: Optional future exploration layer; not required to run, review, or understand the project.

cards.title: What It Is
cards.body: Understand-Anything is optional and is not part of the project runtime, pipeline, training workflow, or deployment process.

cards.title: How To Generate Later
cards.body: Run /understand --language en, then /understand-dashboard in an environment where Understand-Anything is available.

cards.title: Current State
cards.body: This repo may not include .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json. If generated later, it can become an additional exploration layer.

cards.title: Primary Docs Remain
cards.body: README.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/repository_map.md, and this static explorer remain the primary documentation.

important_files.label: Primary Overview
important_files.note: Human-curated documentation remains first.

important_files.label: Static Explorer
important_files.note: This docs-only page.

docs.label: Codebase Knowledge Graph Docs
docs.note: Optional Understand-Anything placeholder and future exploration guide.

warnings: No parser for .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json is implemented in this phase.
Do not require Understand-Anything for normal project operation.
